message:
fix(channel): prevent double-panic abort in watch channel Drop impls

Replace `.expect("watch lock poisoned")` calls in `Sender::drop` and
`Receiver::drop` with defensive `if let Ok(...)` locking. When a mutex
is already poisoned during stack unwinding (e.g. a panic occurred while
the lock was held), calling `.expect()` inside `drop()` would trigger a
second panic, which Rust converts to an immediate process abort. This is
especially problematic in test harnesses and multi-threaded code where
panic recovery (catch_unwind) is expected to work.

Sender::drop changes:
- Lock `sender_dropped` with `if let Ok(...)`, bail on poison
- Collect all wakers from the `waiters` mutex into a SmallVec before
  waking them, so wakers execute outside the lock scope
- On poison, return early instead of panicking

Receiver::drop changes:
- Lock `receiver_count` with `if let Ok(...)` instead of `.expect()`
- Lock `waiters` with `if let Ok(...)` for stale waiter cleanup

Add two regression tests:
- `sender_drop_on_poisoned_mutex_does_not_panic`: intentionally poisons
  the `sender_dropped` mutex, then verifies `drop(tx)` completes without
  panic
- `receiver_drop_on_poisoned_mutex_does_not_panic`: intentionally poisons
  the `receiver_count` mutex, then verifies `drop(rx)` completes without
  panic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
